findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is styled as h1 with text-decoration: underline, which is acceptable. However, the section headings (I, II, III, IV) are rendered as h2, which is appropriate. The heading hierarchy is reasonable.
findings.evidence: h1: MEMORANDUM, h2: I. History/Background, h2: II. March 1, 2016..., h2: III. Written Public Comment Summary, h2: IV. Attachments — all match PDF structure.

findings.description: The footer text (State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Resources...) appears once in the HTML but also appears as a header in the source PDF on each page. This is acceptable consolidation, not problematic duplication.
findings.evidence: Footer block appears once in HTML.

findings.description: There is a nested <main> element inside the outer <main>, which is an HTML structural issue but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> wrapping the entire body content, and another <main> starting after the header.

findings.description: The section heading in the PDF is 'Written Public Comment Summary' (page 3) but in section II the oral comments summary paragraph introduces what should be oral comments as 'Written comments received during the public comment period' — this matches the PDF exactly and is not an error in the HTML.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML contain this same text in section II.
